item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A coffee percolator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote. It's interesting kind of interesting but it just seems too random and unconnected. It's a coffee machine, but also a boat, and also could potentially end the world, even though we could find it before it does permanent damage by looking for where a gigantic flood of coffee is coming from. It doesn't really seem to fit together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Took a while to choose my opinion on this, but I like it. Didnt notice the hints of the possible mental effect, but did enjoy it when it was pointed out. Now, the question is;
"Is the rowboat a coffee machine, or is the coffee machine a rowboat?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no such percolator has been found in any national ship registry.

this secured a smile.
+1

item.title: Reminds me a lot of other SCPS
item.content:encoded:
Dont much like this one.
Downvoted, but I do enjoy the note about the algae.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 to the article and +1 to this dumb pun. +1s for everyone!

item.title: For the sake of argument...
item.content:encoded:
If SCP-1539 is responsible, it would imply that the boat itself is an intelligent entity that once considered itself a rowboat, now considers itself a coffee machine, and is physically capable of functioning as either one. Perhaps a shape-shifter with enough "muscle memory" to still revert to the shape of a rowboat, but believing itself to be a coffee machine due to 1539's effect, and thus adapting to function as one. (It still works as a rowboat because it's still shaped like a rowboat.)

…I feel like re-reading that should confuse me more than it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for some reason i feel that this is the perfect example of an scp. The most important part of an SCP is the feeling and thoughts that you walk away with. Thinking thoughts like "how would I contain this?" "Where did this come from and why?" and "In what ways could this be used (possibly in conjunction with other SCP's) to benefit the foundation and your own life if you could somehow obtain it?" This gives me all of these thoughts and I love it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I find myself amused by something that can cause end-of-the-world-via-coffee and also is/isn't a rowboat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah! Criticism rescinded in that case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The key word is "eventually." It was left out for 9 years and only managed to destroy the ecosystem of a single lake. Given enough time, it would cause a lot more damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no such percolator has been found in any national ship registry.

If that's a meta effect, shouldn't the article be tagged with "meta"?

As there is no limit to the amount of atmospheric moisture SCP-1786 can condense and brew, if exposed to the outside world it would eventually lead to an NK-class scenario involving the conversion of all available water into coffee.

This doesn't seem plausible to me, unless it can spew out an amazing number of liters per second. If it pours coffee out at the rate of a normal percolator, that will make a certain volume of air around the SCP very dry, but it won't get too big. Plus the coffee itself will evaporate and return vapor to the immediate area, unless the coffee has the anomalous effect of being immune to evaporation. If it was evaporation proof, that would be some cause for concern.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It used to be a rowboat. But now it only looks like one.

And it's Safe-class because as long as they keep the water level below 2cm, it won't start a-brewing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was the exact line that I upvoted on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the reason why it's not just a coffee-making rowboat is because it is only perceived as a rowboat. As in, what looks like a 12 foot long wooden water craft is, in fact, only a 12 inch tall steel and copper electric kettle.

Plus, the line:

no such percolator has been found in any national ship registry

seems to be a hint that there's some sort of mental effect that the device exerts. They seem rather insistent that, despite looks, it's not a boat at all and isn't made out of the kind of materials a boat would be made of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm finding it hard to see why this thing wasn't always just viewed as a rowboat that makes coffee. Also shouldn't this perhaps be Euclid? Especially if it has the ability to use water from human bodies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you explain to me how this would "be great as a -J article"? Because when new users state things like this, it usually means they don't actually get what -J articles or mainlist articles are supposed to accomplish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be great as a -J article. I need to think about it as a regular SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was designed, yes, but it wasn't always both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Real old-school vibe off this, especially the NK-Class line. It maybe loses something for having been designed this way, as opposed to just being a rowboat-shaped coffee maker, but it's still great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Yes it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
wh
what?
is-is it a boat or a coffee percolator?
what the fuck?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This type of inexplicable perceptual weirdness is wondrous. Heartily upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because percolated coffee is awful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, explain to me why we're trying to avoid that scenario again?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the visual effect this causes might have further psychic impact.

+1 for mystery, intrigue, and sheer what-the-frak-ery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just so you're aware, roleplaying or acting like the wiki is real is generally frowned upon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I need this SCP………time to make a request to O5 Command

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no such percolator has been found in any national ship registry.

Upboated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice, simple, and effectively done.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird as hell, but I like it. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, especially the phrase about the NK-scenario.
But how do they know it is materially a coffee percolator when it has the appearance of a rowboat?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm getting massive vibes of SCP-1539.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As there is no limit to the amount of atmospheric moisture SCP-1786 can condense and brew, if exposed to the outside world it would eventually lead to an NK-class scenario involving the conversion of all available water into coffee.

I love how these guys can say that so nonchalantly.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It… it's a coffee maker… but it's a rowboat. And you've gotta believe it's there. And… and, and WHY?

Just take my +1 while I go think about this.
